name: Revive Teammate
description: Revive Teammate is a social or networked mechanic that coordinates multiple players within shared rules.
design_notes: Revive Teammate should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For multiplayer mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use revive teammate as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect revive teammate to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: Players feel coordinated, competitive, or socially expressive within shared rules.

parameters.name: player_count
parameters.description: Number of players supported by the mechanic.

parameters.name: sync_rate
parameters.description: Frequency at which state updates are exchanged or resolved.

parameters.name: failure_timeout
parameters.description: Time before the mechanic handles disconnects or missing responses.

edge_cases: Revive Teammate is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Revive Teammate overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Revive Teammate runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Revive Teammate state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Revive Teammate plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Revive Teammate produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Revive Teammate strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Revive Teammate becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Revive Teammate with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Revive Teammate as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Revive Teammate with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Revive Teammate as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
